Acne and Rosacea Photos.description: A group of chronic inflammatory skin conditions affecting hair follicles and facial blood vessels.
Acne and Rosacea Photos.symptoms: Pimples, blackheads, whiteheads, facial redness, visible blood vessels.
Acne and Rosacea Photos.causes: Hormonal changes, excess oil production, bacteria, genetics, environmental triggers.
Acne and Rosacea Photos.treatment: Topical retinoids, benzoyl peroxide, antibiotics, oral isotretinoin (severe acne), lifestyle modifications.
Acne and Rosacea Photos.advice: Consult a dermatologist if lesions are severe, painful, or leave scars.

Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.description: Precancerous or cancerous skin lesions usually caused by prolonged sun exposure.
Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.symptoms: Rough scaly patches, non-healing sores, pearly bumps, irregular lesions.
Actinic Keratosis Basal Cell Carcinoma a.treatment: Cryotherapy, topical medications, surgical excision, Mohs surgery depending on diagnosis.

Atopic Dermatitis Photos.description: A chronic inflammatory skin disease commonly known as eczema.
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.symptoms: Dry skin, itching, redness, skin thickening.
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.causes: Genetic predisposition, immune dysfunction, environmental allergens.
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.treatment: Moisturizers, topical corticosteroids, calcineurin inhibitors, antihistamines.
Atopic Dermatitis Photos.advice: Avoid scratching and identify possible triggers.

Bullous Disease Photos.description: A group of disorders characterized by fluid-filled blisters.

Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.description: Bacterial infections affecting the skin and underlying tissues.
Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.symptoms: Redness, swelling, warmth, pain, pus-filled lesions.
Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.treatment: Oral or intravenous antibiotics depending on severity.
Cellulitis Impetigo and other Bacterial.advice: Seek medical attention promptly to prevent complications.

Eczema Photos.description: Inflammatory skin disorder causing itching and irritation.
Eczema Photos.treatment: Moisturizers, topical steroids, avoidance of irritants.
Eczema Photos.advice: Keep skin moisturized and avoid harsh soaps.

Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.description: Skin rashes caused by infections or adverse drug reactions.
Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.causes: Viruses, antibiotics, anticonvulsants and other medications.
Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.treatment: Discontinue offending drug under medical supervision, antihistamines, corticosteroids.
Exanthems and Drug Eruptions.advice: Seek urgent care if rash is accompanied by breathing difficulty or blistering.

Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.description: Conditions causing hair thinning or hair loss.
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.symptoms: Patchy hair loss, diffuse thinning, scalp changes.
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.causes: Autoimmune disorders, hormonal imbalance, genetics, stress, nutritional deficiencies.
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.treatment: Minoxidil, finasteride (where appropriate), corticosteroids, nutritional correction.
Hair Loss Photos Alopecia and other Hair.advice: Consult a dermatologist if hair loss is sudden or extensive.

Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.description: Skin manifestations of sexually transmitted viral infections.
Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.causes: Herpes simplex virus, Human papillomavirus and other pathogens.
Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.treatment: Antiviral medications or wart removal depending on the condition.
Herpes HPV and other STDs Photos.advice: Consult a healthcare professional for diagnosis and treatment.

Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.symptoms: White patches, dark patches, uneven skin color.
Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.causes: Autoimmune disorders, sun exposure, inflammation, genetics.
Light Diseases and Disorders of Pigmenta.advice: Avoid excessive sun exposure and use sunscreen.

Lupus and other Connective Tissue diseas.description: Autoimmune diseases affecting skin and connective tissues.
Lupus and other Connective Tissue diseas.advice: Regular follow-up with a rheumatologist or dermatologist is recommended.

Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.description: Pigmented skin lesions ranging from benign moles to malignant melanoma.
Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.symptoms: Changing mole, asymmetry, irregular borders, color variation.
Melanoma Skin Cancer Nevi and Moles.treatment: Surgical removal and oncology referral if melanoma is confirmed.

Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.description: Skin inflammation caused by contact with allergens or irritants.
Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.treatment: Topical steroids, antihistamines, avoidance of trigger.
Poison Ivy Photos and other Contact Derm.advice: Wash exposed skin promptly after contact.

Psoriasis pictures Lichen Planus and rel.description: Chronic inflammatory skin disorders characterized by scaling plaques or purple itchy lesions.
Psoriasis pictures Lichen Planus and rel.treatment: Topical corticosteroids, vitamin D analogues, biologic therapy.
Psoriasis pictures Lichen Planus and rel.advice: Avoid known triggers and follow dermatologist recommendations.

Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.description: Skin diseases caused by parasites, insect bites, or tick-borne infections.
Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.symptoms: Intense itching, burrows, bite marks, rash.
Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.treatment: Permethrin, ivermectin, antibiotics for Lyme disease when indicated.
Scabies Lyme Disease and other Infestati.advice: Treat close contacts if scabies is confirmed.

Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.symptoms: Raised brown or black lesions with a waxy appearance.
Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.treatment: Usually unnecessary unless symptomatic; cryotherapy or removal if needed.
Seborrheic Keratoses and other Benign Tu.advice: Seek evaluation if lesions change rapidly.

Systemic Disease.description: Skin manifestations associated with underlying systemic illnesses.
Systemic Disease.symptoms: Variable rash, ulcers, discoloration depending on the disease.
Systemic Disease.causes: Internal medical conditions such as diabetes, autoimmune diseases, or infections.

Tinea Ringworm Candidiasis and other Fun.description: Superficial fungal infections affecting skin, nails, or mucous membranes.
Tinea Ringworm Candidiasis and other Fun.advice: Keep affected areas clean and dry.

Urticaria Hives.description: Raised itchy welts caused by allergic or non-allergic triggers.
Urticaria Hives.causes: Allergic reactions, infections, medications, unknown triggers.
Urticaria Hives.advice: Seek emergency care if swelling affects breathing.

Vascular Tumors.description: Benign or malignant growths arising from blood vessels.
Vascular Tumors.symptoms: Red, purple, or bluish skin lesions.
Vascular Tumors.treatment: Observation, laser therapy, surgery depending on type.

Vasculitis Photos.description: Inflammation of blood vessels affecting the skin.

Warts Molluscum and other Viral Infectio.symptoms: Small bumps, rough growths, pearly papules.
Warts Molluscum and other Viral Infectio.treatment: Cryotherapy, salicylic acid, curettage, or observation.
Warts Molluscum and other Viral Infectio.advice: Avoid sharing personal items and avoid scratching lesions.
